LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT MV TRANSPORTATION, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS CONTINUED TO OCTOBER 29, 2012 AT 9:30 AM TO GIVE LATHEM BONEM THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. LATHEM BONEM MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE OPPOSITION PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302 WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE NO LATER THAN OCTOBER 19, 2012 AT 4:00 PM, OR THE MOTION WILL BE GRANTED. THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING AND REPLY PAPERS. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED.
MINI MINUTES FOR OCT-19-2012 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302
MINI MINUTES FOR OCT-29-2012 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MV TRANSPORTATION, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED BECAUSE DEFENDANT FAILED TO MAINTAIN ITS INITIAL BURDEN. DEFENDANT FAILS TO SHOW THAT IT WAS AN INTENDED THIRD PARTY BENEFICIARY OF THE BONEM/SHEFMAN SETTLEMENT. DEFENDANT FAILS TO SHOW THAT RES JUDICATA OR COLLATERAL ESTOPPEL APPLIES. THE TWO ACTIONS DID NOT INVOLVE THE SAME PRIMARY RIGHT, SAME PARTIES OR PRIVIES, SAME CAUSES OF ACTION AND THE SAME ISSUES WERE NOT ADJUDICATED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR # 7077

Mtn To Dismiss Entire Action
Off Calendar Apr-28-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Arlen Litman-Cleper, Esq. of Harrington Foxx Dubrow & Carter LLP, Tel. 415.288.6600, for defendant and moving party;
Gregory C. Cattermole, Esq. of Carcione Cattermole Dolinski Stucky Markowitz & Carcione, Tel. 650.367.6811, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. After argument, the Court adopted the tentative rulng. DEFENDANT MV TRANSPORTATION, INC.'S Motion To Dismiss Entire Action is Denied. Defendant has not established that Plaintiff has unreasonably or inexcusably delayed to an extent that would mandate the drastic remedy of involuntary dismissal. The evidence establishes that the parties have exchanged discovery, participated in settlement discussions, and filed/defended a motion for summary judgment. Order signed. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR# 5976.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-29-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Gregory J. Sowder, Esq. of Harrington, Foxx, Dubrow & Canter, LLP, Tel. 415.288.6600, for defendant and moving party;
Gregory C. Cattermole, Esq. of Carcione, Cattermole, Dolinski, Stuky, Markowitz & Carcione, for plaintiff and opposing party.

The matter was argued. The court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT MV TRANSPORTATION, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied because defendant failed to maintain its initial burden. Defendant fails to show that it was an intended third party beneficiary of the Bonem/Shefman settlement. Defendant fails to show that res judicata or collateral estoppel applies. The two actions did not involve the same primary right, same parties or privies, same causes of action and the same issues were not adjudicated. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-19-2012 Continued To Oct-29-12 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter.
RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT MV TRANSPORTATION, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is continued to October 29, 2012 at 9:30 am to give Lathem Bonem the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Lathem Bonem must deliver courtesy copies of the opposition papers to Department 302 with a cover letter stating the new hearing date no later than October 19, 2012 at 4:00 pm, or the motion will be granted. The court has courtesy copies of the moving and reply papers. Judge: Harold Kahn Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.
